title: CVE-2025-24813 Tomcat RCE via partial PUT (CRITICAL CVSS 9.8)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 is affected by CVE-2025-24813 (CVSS 9.8). Partial PUT + default servlet write enabled + file-based session persistence allows RCE. Fixed in 10.1.35. The generated pom.xml in the build artifact locks Tomcat to an old version via the Spring Boot 3.x BOM bundled with plugwerk-api-endpoint. The main server module uses Spring Boot 4.0.5 which should pull a newer Tomcat — verify the runtime version is >= 10.1.35 and purge the stale generated pom.xml from the repository.
recommendation: 1) Confirm the actual runtime Tomcat version shipped in the fat JAR is >= 10.1.35 via ./gradlew :plugwerk-server:plugwerk-server-backend:dependencies | grep tomcat-embed-core. 2) If the generated pom.xml is stale, exclude it from Trivy scans with a .trivyignore entry or add trivy:ignore comment. 3) If the API-endpoint module still pulls Spring Boot 3.x, upgrade its BOM dependency.

title: CVE-2026-29145 Tomcat CLIENT_CERT authentication bypass (CRITICAL CVSS 9.1)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 is affected by CVE-2026-29145 (CVSS 9.1). CLIENT_CERT soft-fail misconfiguration allows authentication bypass. Fixed in 10.1.53. Same root cause as SEC-001: stale generated pom.xml from the plugwerk-api-endpoint module.
recommendation: Upgrade plugwerk-api-endpoint module to use a Spring Boot BOM that resolves Tomcat >= 10.1.53. Confirm runtime Tomcat version; add .trivyignore for the generated pom.xml once confirmed runtime is safe.

title: CVE-2023-6378 logback-core 1.4.11 DoS via receiver serialization (HIGH CVSS 7.1)
description: ch.qos.logback:logback-core 1.4.11 and logback-classic 1.4.11 are affected by CVE-2023-6378. A poisoned data payload sent to the logback receiver component can cause DoS. Fixed in logback 1.4.12 / 1.3.12. The stale generated pom.xml in plugwerk-api-endpoint pins logback to 1.4.11 via the old Spring Boot BOM.
recommendation: Upgrade the plugwerk-api-endpoint BOM to Spring Boot >= 3.2.x (which includes logback >= 1.4.12). Verify the runtime server classpath does not include any 1.4.11 logback JARs.

title: CVE-2024-50379 and CVE-2024-56337 Tomcat RCE via TOCTOU in JSP (HIGH CVSS 9.8 declared HIGH in Trivy)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 is affected by both CVE-2024-50379 and CVE-2024-56337. TOCTOU race condition during JSP compilation on case-insensitive file systems (write-enabled default servlet, non-default config) allows RCE. Fixed in 10.1.34. Requires default servlet write to be enabled (non-default). Risk is low on Plugwerk because JSP and the default write servlet are not used, but the dependency version must still be updated.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat to >= 10.1.34 (already done in the main server module via Spring Boot 4.0.5). Remediate the stale generated pom.xml in plugwerk-api-endpoint.

title: CVE-2023-46589 Tomcat HTTP request smuggling via trailer headers (HIGH CVSS 7.5)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2023-46589. Malformed HTTP trailer headers can cause Tomcat to parse a single request as multiple, enabling request smuggling behind a reverse proxy. Fixed in 10.1.16.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat to >= 10.1.16 (covered by upgrading the api-endpoint BOM as per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2024-34750 Tomcat HTTP/2 stream miscounting DoS (HIGH CVSS 7.5 / CVSSv4 8.7)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2024-34750. Excessive HTTP/2 headers cause a stream miscounting bug, leading to connections remaining open indefinitely (DoS). Fixed in 10.1.25.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat to >= 10.1.25 (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2025-48988 Tomcat multipart upload DoS (HIGH CVSS 7.5 / CVSSv4 8.7)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2025-48988. Unbounded resource allocation in multipart upload handling leads to DoS. Fixed in 10.1.42.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat to >= 10.1.42 (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2025-48989 Tomcat HTTP/2 MadeYouReset DoS (HIGH CVSS 7.5)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2025-48989. Improper resource release on HTTP/2 control frames allows DoS (MadeYouReset). Fixed in 10.1.44.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat to >= 10.1.44 (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2025-52520 Tomcat multipart upload integer overflow DoS (HIGH CVSS 7.5)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2025-52520. Integer overflow allows bypass of multipart size limits leading to DoS. Fixed in 10.1.43.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat to >= 10.1.43 (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2025-53506 Tomcat HTTP/2 uncontrolled resource consumption DoS (HIGH CVSS 7.5)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2025-53506. If an HTTP/2 client does not acknowledge the initial settings frame, Tomcat consumes unbounded resources. Fixed in 10.1.43.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat to >= 10.1.43 (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2025-55752 Tomcat directory traversal via rewrite with possible RCE (HIGH CVSS 7.5)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2025-55752. Regression in URL rewrite normalization allows path traversal bypassing security constraints including /WEB-INF/. If PUT is enabled, RCE is possible. Fixed in 10.1.45.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat to >= 10.1.45 (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2026-24734 Tomcat OCSP revocation bypass (HIGH CVSS 7.5)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2026-24734. Incomplete OCSP response verification allows certificate revocation bypass. Fixed in 10.1.52. Impact limited to deployments using mTLS with an OCSP responder.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat to >= 10.1.52 (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2026-34483 Tomcat JsonAccessLogValve information disclosure (HIGH CVSS 7.5)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2026-34483. Improper encoding in JsonAccessLogValve can leak sensitive data. Fixed in 10.1.54.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat to >= 10.1.54 (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2026-34487 Tomcat Kubernetes bearer token in logs (HIGH CVSS 7.5)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2026-34487. Cloud membership clustering component logs Kubernetes bearer token. Fixed in 10.1.54. Impact limited to Kubernetes deployments using Tomcat clustering.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat to >= 10.1.54 (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2025-22235 Spring Boot EndpointRequest.to() creates wrong matcher (HIGH CVSS 7.3)
description: spring-boot affected by CVE-2025-22235. EndpointRequest.to() creates a null/** matcher if the referenced actuator endpoint is disabled or not exposed, potentially bypassing security rules on /null paths. Fixed in spring-boot 3.4.5 / 3.3.11. Affects Plugwerk only if EndpointRequest.to() is used in security config for disabled endpoints.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Boot BOM to >= 3.4.5. Audit the security config for usage of EndpointRequest.to() referencing non-exposed endpoints.

title: CVE-2025-41249 Spring Framework annotation detection bypass for @EnableMethodSecurity (HIGH CVSS 7.5)
description: spring-core affected by CVE-2025-41249. Annotation detection may fail for methods in type hierarchies with parameterized super types, breaking @EnableMethodSecurity authorization. Fixed in spring-core 6.2.11.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Framework to >= 6.2.11 (via Spring Boot BOM upgrade).

title: CVE-2024-22243 / CVE-2024-22259 / CVE-2024-22262 Spring UriComponentsBuilder SSRF/open-redirect (HIGH CVSS 8.1)
description: spring-web affected by CVE-2024-22243, CVE-2024-22259, CVE-2024-22262. UriComponentsBuilder host validation can be bypassed enabling open-redirect or SSRF. Fixed in spring-web 6.1.6. Affects Plugwerk if UriComponentsBuilder is used to parse user-supplied URLs with host validation.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Framework to >= 6.1.6 (via Spring Boot BOM upgrade). Audit usages of UriComponentsBuilder.fromUriString(userInput) in the codebase.

title: CVE-2023-34053 Spring MVC + Micrometer DoS (HIGH CVSS 7.5)
description: spring-webmvc affected by CVE-2023-34053. Specially crafted requests against Spring MVC with ObservationRegistry can cause DoS. Fixed in spring-webmvc 6.0.14.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Framework to >= 6.0.14 (covered by BOM upgrade).

title: CVE-2024-38816 / CVE-2024-38819 Spring WebMvc.fn path traversal (HIGH CVSS 7.5)
description: spring-webmvc affected by CVE-2024-38816 and CVE-2024-38819. RouterFunctions + FileSystemResource allows path traversal. Fixed in 6.1.14. Not exploitable on Tomcat with default Spring Security HTTP Firewall enabled, but dependency must be updated.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Framework to >= 6.1.14 (covered by BOM upgrade). Confirm Spring Security HTTP Firewall is active.

title: CVE-2022-1471 SnakeYAML unsafe deserialization RCE (HIGH CVSS 8.3)
description: org.yaml:snakeyaml affected by CVE-2022-1471. The unsafe Constructor() class can be exploited for RCE if attacker-controlled YAML is parsed. Fixed in snakeyaml 2.0. Spring Boot 4.0.x should include snakeyaml >= 2.0; presence in the stale generated pom.xml is a build artifact issue.
recommendation: Upgrade the plugwerk-api-endpoint module BOM. Confirm runtime classpath uses snakeyaml >= 2.0.

title: CVE-2024-12798 logback ACE via JaninoEventEvaluator (MEDIUM)
description: logback-core 1.4.11 affected by CVE-2024-12798. Attacker with write access to logback config can execute arbitrary code via JaninoEventEvaluator. Requires existing privilege. Fixed in 1.5.13.
recommendation: Upgrade logback (via Spring Boot BOM upgrade to >= 3.2.x).

title: CVE-2025-11226 logback conditional ACE (MEDIUM)
description: logback-core 1.4.11 affected by CVE-2025-11226. ACE via conditional configuration file processing (requires Janino + Spring on classpath and write access to config). Fixed in 1.5.19.
recommendation: Upgrade logback (via Spring Boot BOM upgrade).

title: GHSA-72hv-8253-57qq jackson-core async parser maxNumberLength bypass DoS (MEDIUM)
description: com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-core 2.15.2 affected by GHSA-72hv-8253-57qq. The async (non-blocking) JSON parser ignores the maxNumberLength constraint, enabling memory/CPU exhaustion DoS. Fixed in 2.18.6 / 2.21.1. This is the annotations-only jackson-core 2.x dependency in the stale generated pom; the main server uses jackson 3.1.0 (tools.jackson group).
recommendation: Upgrade jackson-annotations to a version where the accompanying jackson-core is >= 2.18.6, or pin com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-core >= 2.18.6 explicitly in the api-endpoint module.

title: CVE-2023-41080 Tomcat open redirect in FORM auth (MEDIUM CVSS 6.1)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2023-41080. Open redirect in FORM authentication (ROOT web application only). Fixed in 10.1.13. Not exploitable unless FORM auth is used.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2023-42795 Tomcat information leak via recycled objects (MEDIUM CVSS 5.3)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2023-42795. Recycling error causes request/response data to leak across requests. Fixed in 10.1.14.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2023-44487 HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack DoS (MEDIUM CVSS 5.3)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2023-44487 (Rapid Reset). Exploited in the wild 2023. Fixed in 10.1.14.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2023-45648 Tomcat HTTP trailer header request smuggling (MEDIUM CVSS 5.3)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2023-45648. Invalid trailer header causes request smuggling. Fixed in 10.1.14.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2024-24549 Tomcat HTTP/2 header handling DoS (MEDIUM CVSS 7.5)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2024-24549. Requests exceeding header limits do not reset the HTTP/2 stream immediately, enabling DoS. Fixed in 10.1.19.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2025-31650 Tomcat invalid HTTP priority header memory leak DoS (MEDIUM)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2025-31650. Memory leak from invalid HTTP priority headers can trigger OOM DoS. Fixed in 10.1.40.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2025-49124 Tomcat Windows installer untrusted search path (MEDIUM — not applicable)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2025-49124. Windows-installer-only issue (icacls.exe path not specified). Plugwerk runs on Linux in Docker; this is not exploitable in the deployed context.
recommendation: Mark as false-positive for Docker/Linux deployments. Upgrade Tomcat anyway as part of general BOM upgrade.

title: CVE-2025-49125 Tomcat security constraint bypass for pre/post-resources (MEDIUM)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2025-49125. PreResources or PostResources mounted off-root can be accessed via paths not covered by security constraints. Fixed in 10.1.42.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001). Confirm no PreResources/PostResources are used.

title: CVE-2025-66614 Tomcat client cert bypass via SNI/Host mismatch (MEDIUM CVSS 9.1 v3)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2025-66614. SNI extension hostname not validated against HTTP Host header; allows client cert auth bypass with multiple virtual hosts. Fixed in 10.1.50. Exploitable only if multiple virtual hosts with different mTLS requirements are configured.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2026-25854 Tomcat open redirect via LoadBalancerDrainingValve (MEDIUM)
description: tomcat-embed-core 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2026-25854. Open redirect via LoadBalancerDrainingValve. Fixed in 10.1.53. Exploitable only if LoadBalancerDrainingValve is configured.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2024-23672 Tomcat WebSocket DoS (MEDIUM CVSS 6.3)
description: tomcat-embed-websocket 10.1.12 affected by CVE-2024-23672. WebSocket clients can keep connections open indefinitely causing resource consumption. Fixed in 10.1.19.
recommendation: Upgrade Tomcat (covered by BOM upgrade per SEC-001).

title: CVE-2024-38820 Spring DataBinder disallowedFields locale-dependent bypass (MEDIUM CVSS 5.3)
description: spring-web affected by CVE-2024-38820. String.toLowerCase() locale exceptions may allow disallowedFields patterns to be bypassed. Fixed in spring-web 6.1.14.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Framework (covered by BOM upgrade).

title: CVE-2024-38809 Spring Framework ETag conditional request DoS (MEDIUM CVSS 5.3)
description: spring-web affected by CVE-2024-38809. Parsing ETags from If-Match or If-None-Match headers can cause DoS. Fixed in spring-web 6.1.12.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Framework (covered by BOM upgrade).

title: CVE-2025-41234 Spring Framework reflected file download via ContentDisposition (MEDIUM CVSS 6.5)
description: spring-web affected by CVE-2025-41234. ContentDisposition.Builder#filename(String Charset) with user-supplied input and non-ASCII charset enables RFD attack. Fixed in spring-web 6.1.21.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Framework (covered by BOM upgrade). Audit all usages of ContentDisposition.Builder#filename(String Charset).

title: CVE-2025-41242 Spring MVC path traversal on non-compliant Servlet containers (MEDIUM CVSS 5.9)
description: spring-webmvc affected by CVE-2025-41242. Path traversal on Servlet containers that do not reject suspicious sequences. Apache Tomcat with default configuration is NOT affected. Fixed in spring-webmvc 6.2.10.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Framework (covered by BOM upgrade). Running on Tomcat with defaults mitigates this.

title: CVE-2026-22737 Spring MVC scripting engine template information disclosure (MEDIUM CVSS 5.9)
description: spring-webmvc affected by CVE-2026-22737. Java scripting engine enabled template views (JRuby/Jython) can disclose files outside configured locations. Fixed in spring-webmvc 6.2.17. Impact: none if JSP scripting engine templates are not used.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Framework (covered by BOM upgrade). Confirm no JRuby/Jython ScriptTemplateViewResolver is configured.

title: License notice: logback-classic EPL-1.0 and LGPL-2.1-only
description: Trivy flags logback-classic and logback-core under EPL-1.0 (reciprocal) and LGPL-2.1-only (restricted). LGPL-2.1 is compatible with AGPL-3.0 when dynamically linked (which is the case here — logback is a runtime dependency pulled transitively). EPL-1.0 is also compatible under the same terms. No action required but should be reviewed by legal counsel for any dual-licensing commercial offering.
recommendation: Confirm dynamic linking (JAR on classpath) satisfies LGPL-2.1 requirements. Document in THIRD-PARTY-LICENSES.

title: License notice: jakarta.annotation-api EPL-2.0 and GPL-2.0-with-classpath-exception
description: Trivy flags jakarta.annotation:jakarta.annotation-api under EPL-2.0 (reciprocal) and GPL-2.0-with-classpath-exception (restricted). The classpath exception means usage on the classpath is permitted regardless of the license of Plugwerk itself. No action required.
recommendation: Document in THIRD-PARTY-LICENSES file.

title: Docker base image eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine uses floating tag (no SHA digest)
description: The Dockerfile uses FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine without a pinned SHA-256 digest. This means the image can change silently between builds if the upstream tag is updated, introducing untested changes or supply chain risk. Hadolint would normally catch this (DL3007) but the hadolint scan was reported clean — this finding is from direct code review.
recommendation: Pin the base image to a specific digest: FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine@sha256:<digest>. Update the digest as part of the regular dependency maintenance cycle.

title: Dockerfile missing HEALTHCHECK directive
description: The Dockerfile does not include a HEALTHCHECK instruction. Without it Docker cannot automatically detect that the application is unhealthy and orchestrators cannot reliably restart failed containers. The docker-compose.yml provides a healthcheck at the compose level only.
recommendation: Add HEALTHCHECK NONE or a real health check: HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=30s --retries=3 CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/actuator/health | grep -q '\status\":\"UP\"' || exit 1"

title: docker-compose.yml PLUGWERK_AUTH_ADMIN_PASSWORD uses empty default (silent insecure start)
description: Line 29 sets PLUGWERK_AUTH_ADMIN_PASSWORD: ${PLUGWERK_AUTH_ADMIN_PASSWORD:-} — if the environment variable is not set the placeholder resolves to an empty string rather than failing fast. The application generates a random password and logs it once at startup which is documented behavior but the operator may not notice. PLUGWERK_JWT_SECRET and PLUGWERK_ENCRYPTION_KEY correctly use the :? fail-fast syntax. POSTGRES_PASSWORD is hardcoded to 'plugwerk' (acceptable only for local dev compose).
recommendation: Consider also applying the :? fail-fast syntax to PLUGWERK_AUTH_ADMIN_PASSWORD in the dist docker-compose.yml so that operators are explicitly forced to set it. Document the random-generation fallback clearly in the README.

title: docker-compose Quick Start snippet contains weak example credentials
description: The Quick Start section in dockerhub-readme.md (line 50-52) shows PLUGWERK_JWT_SECRET: change-me-min-32-characters-long! and PLUGWERK_ENCRYPTION_KEY: exactly16charss and PLUGWERK_AUTH_ADMIN_PASSWORD: admin as example values. Operators who copy-paste this without changing the values will run production with known weak credentials. These are documentation examples not real leaked secrets (see SEC-FP-001) but they pose a social-engineering / copy-paste risk.
recommendation: Add a prominent warning in bold immediately above the snippet: WARNING: Replace ALL secret values before deploying to production. Consider replacing the inline secret values with <GENERATE WITH: openssl rand -base64 32> placeholder text.

title: CI workflow uses floating action references without SHA pinning
description: ci.yml (and other workflows) reference actions via floating tags: actions/checkout@v4 actions/setup-java@v4 actions/setup-node@v4 gradle/actions/setup-gradle@v4 actions/upload-artifact@v4. If any of these tags are moved or the upstream repository is compromised a supply-chain attack can execute arbitrary code in the CI pipeline with the GITHUB_TOKEN.
recommendation: Pin all actions to a full commit SHA: e.g. actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 (v4.2.2). Use a tool like Dependabot (actions) or pin-github-action to automate.

title: release.yml uses peter-evans/dockerhub-description@v4 and peter-evans/repository-dispatch@v3 without SHA pinning
description: release.yml references third-party actions at floating tags: peter-evans/dockerhub-description@v4 peter-evans/repository-dispatch@v3 docker/setup-qemu-action@v3 docker/setup-buildx-action@v3 docker/login-action@v3 docker/metadata-action@v5 docker/build-push-action@v6. These are higher-risk than first-party actions because they are maintained by third parties.
recommendation: Pin all third-party actions to commit SHAs. Use Dependabot to keep them current.

title: cla.yml uses pull_request_target with overly broad permissions (contents: write pull-requests: write) and a third-party action at a non-SHA-pinned tag
description: cla.yml triggers on pull_request_target (which runs with the target branch context and can access secrets) and grants contents: write pull-requests: write statuses: write actions: write. The action contributor-assistant/github-action@v2.6.1 is a floating tag reference. A compromised upstream action or a carefully crafted PR could potentially abuse these elevated permissions. The CLA assistant writes a JSON file (signatures/cla.json) to the cla-signatures branch which requires write access but the scope could be narrowed.
recommendation: 1) Pin contributor-assistant/github-action to a full commit SHA. 2) Scope permissions to the minimum required: the action only needs pull-requests: write statuses: write and the CLA_TOKEN for writing the signatures branch — remove actions: write if not needed by the action. 3) Review whether the action performs any untrusted code execution from PR branches (pull_request_target risk).

title: security.yml Trivy scan uses aquasecurity/trivy-action@v0.35.0 (floating minor tag)
description: security.yml references aquasecurity/trivy-action@v0.35.0. While this is more specific than @main it is not a commit SHA and could be silently updated.
recommendation: Pin to full commit SHA.

title: Actuator exposes prometheus endpoint without authentication requirement documented
description: application.yml exposes three actuator endpoints: health info prometheus (management.endpoints.web.exposure.include). The health endpoint correctly uses show-details: when-authorized. The prometheus endpoint exposes metrics including JVM heap size thread counts and HTTP request counts which can leak operational intelligence to unauthenticated callers if not protected by the Spring Security config (owned by SBS lane). This entry flags the exposure; SBS lane should confirm the prometheus endpoint is behind authentication or accessible only from internal network.
recommendation: Verify that the /actuator/prometheus endpoint requires authentication in the Spring Security config. If the endpoint is intended for a Prometheus scraper consider requiring a dedicated scraper token or limiting access by IP at the reverse proxy level.

title: No spring.servlet.multipart explicit config observed — relying on MultipartConfig bean
description: Comment in application.yml (line 22-25) states that multipart limits are set programmatically at bean-creation time from the application_setting table with a hard 1 GB ceiling. This is correct per ADR-0016. No misconfiguration detected. Recording as info for completeness.
recommendation: No action required. Ensure ADR-0016 1 GB ceiling is enforced in MultipartConfig.kt and that the ceiling cannot be overridden via user-supplied settings.

title: OpenAPI top-level security: [] intentionally overridden per-operation — design is correct
description: The OpenAPI spec sets a top-level security: [] (no global default) and every operation explicitly declares its own security requirements. All authenticated endpoints declare bearer/apiKey schemes. Public endpoints (/api/v1/config GET /namespaces/{ns}/plugins.json catalog endpoints update-check) are correctly marked security: []. No accidentally-public operations detected.
recommendation: No action required. Continue to ensure every new operation explicitly declares its security scheme.

title: hadolint scan returned clean — no Dockerfile lint issues
description: The hadolint.txt artifact contains no findings confirming no obvious Dockerfile anti-patterns (RUN apt-get without no-install-recommends ADD vs COPY shell form ENTRYPOINT etc.). The non-root user USER plugwerk is present. Reporting as confirmed clean pass.
recommendation: No action required.

title: Gitleaks finding SEC-FP-001: PLUGWERK_ENCRYPTION_KEY: exactly16charss — confirmed false positive
description: File docker/dockerhub-readme.md line 51 contains PLUGWERK_ENCRYPTION_KEY: exactly16charss inside a documentation Quick Start compose snippet. The value is a deliberate placeholder example (16 chars exactly to match the AES-128 requirement) not a real secret. Entropy score 3.51 is low. The surrounding context (lines 49-52) clearly shows these are documentation examples that should be replaced. Not a real credential.
recommendation: No rotation required. Consider adding a gitleaks:allow comment or .gitleaksignore rule to suppress future false positives in doc files.

title: Gitleaks finding SEC-FP-002: PLUGWERK_JWT_SECRET=REDACTED in planning doc — confirmed false positive
description: File docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-15-issue-217-docker-hub-publish.md line 331 contains PLUGWERK_JWT_SECRET= inside an implementation planning document. The value is a placeholder used to illustrate the compose setup being planned. Entropy score 4.0. Not a real deployed secret.
recommendation: No rotation required. Add a .gitleaksignore rule for docs/superpowers/ to suppress future FPs.

title: Gitleaks finding SEC-FP-003: PLUGWERK_ENCRYPTION_KEY=REDACTED in planning doc — confirmed false positive
description: File docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-15-issue-217-docker-hub-publish.md line 332 contains PLUGWERK_ENCRYPTION_KEY= adjacent to the JWT_SECRET placeholder (line 331). Same planning document context. Not a real credential.
recommendation: No rotation required. Covered by .gitleaksignore rule for docs/superpowers/ (see SEC-FP-002).

title: Gitleaks finding SEC-FP-004: password JSON field in test fixture — confirmed false positive
description: File AdminUserControllerTest.kt line 168 contains a password field value embedded in a JSON request body string used as a test fixture. The commit message confirms this was added as part of updating password fixtures to meet the new 12-character minimum. Entropy score 3.55. This is a test-only hardcoded password in a clearly labelled test class not a production credential.
recommendation: No rotation required. Test fixture passwords are expected. Consider using a named test constant for improved readability.

title: Gitleaks finding SEC-FP-005: encryptionKey test fixture in PlugwerkPropertiesValidatorTest — confirmed false positive
description: File PlugwerkPropertiesValidatorTest.kt line 72 contains encryptionKey = followed by a 16-character test value used to exercise the properties validator. Entropy 3.625. This is a unit test class testing that startup validation rejects insecure default values — the key here is a deliberate test input not a real secret.
recommendation: No rotation required.

title: Gitleaks finding SEC-FP-006: encryptionKey test fixture in AuthPropertiesValidatorTest — confirmed false positive
description: File AuthPropertiesValidatorTest.kt line 61 contains encryptionKey = in a unit test for the auth properties validator. Entropy 3.625. Identical pattern to SEC-FP-005 — a test fixture for the predecessor validator class (renamed to PlugwerkPropertiesValidator). Not a real credential.
recommendation: No rotation required.
